title: 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G]

fields.vision: Georgetown technique registry is a method-authority packet covering all 50 SSP courses and thousands of course-derived technique lines.
fields.mechanism: It preserves U.S. National Security Policy through Intelligence and War, including the Research-Methods, Statistics, SAT, Operations Research, Game-Theory, Disruptive-Analytics, Forecasting, Modeling-Complexity, Political-Analyst, and Writing-for-Intel spine; old-to-new SEST crosswalks, Scenario Registry tags, Evidence Packet tags, Interagency Roles, Scoring, and Gates connect the registry to capstone and future method work.
fields.source_utilization: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need method-passage extraction and claim-to-source linkage. Source pool: 60 deduplicated local title candidates; 63 catalogue support rows. Examples: Improving Intelligence Analysis; Leading Intelligence Analysis. Research design, evidence evaluation, forecasting, and judgment. Use source library method and reasoning sources as public method and source background. Vision: Make methodology pages legible as research-design, evidence-control, and interpretive-method systems. Mechanism: Extract argument, warrant, close reading, corpus method, reasoning, evidence, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Strengthen source governance, evidence workbench, capstone, and Nexus method pages. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact methodological passages for claim-control upgrades. Source pool: 129 deduplicated local title candidates; 136 catalogue support rows. Examples: Argumentation; From Human Reasoning to Belief. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision, mechanism, source-use, plan...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Look up a Georgetown SSP course by old OR new SEST code in index.csv. 2. Open the corresponding Part_ _SEST_ _*.md for the full lossless technique inventory. 3. Use capstone_plugs.csv to pull every technique tagged (say) [G] across all 50 courses for a Quality-Gates library. 4. Use technique_lines.csv for full-text search or boolean-plug filtering in Excel/pandas. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None — this is a corpus package, no scripts. Rendered forms (.xlsx, .csv, .json) are generated outputs for Sheets/Excel/programmatic consumption.
fields.remaining_gaps: Read the entry-point materials, classify the packet's authority level, and decide whether the next move is build, consolidation, archival retention, or public synthesis.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A lossless, per-course technique registry covering all 50 SSP courses at Georgetown (from SEST 5100 U.S. National Security Policy through SEST 6718 Intelligence & War, including the entire Research-Methods/Stats/SAT/OR/Game-Theory/Disruptive-Analytics/Forecasting/Modeling-Complexity/Political-Analyst/Writing-for-Intel spine). Each "Part" file is an operator-library that enumerates every tradecraft line in a course — roughly 65 to 440 techniques per course — with anchor verification (the author explicitly pins Registrar crosswalks for old-to-new SEST numbering so as not to hallucinate syllabi), then explodes into a numbered, headed inventory. The registry is capstone-plug-ready: a parallel capstone_plugs.csv/.json maps technique lines into five capstone work product categories — [SR] Scenario Registry, [EP] Evidence Packets, [IR] Interagency Roles, [SC] Scoring, [G] Gates (QA/decision thresholds) — so any of the ~7,500+ technique lines can be...
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The mechanism is method operationalization: name the analytic technique, define its use condition, connect it to a case or judgment problem, record failure modes, and decide whether it is a live method page or a reference packet.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Georgetown technique registry is a method-authority packet covering all 50 SSP courses and thousands of course-derived technique lines.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It preserves U.S. National Security Policy through Intelligence and War, including the Research-Methods, Statistics, SAT, Operations Research, Game-Theory, Disruptive-Analytics, Forecasting, Modeling-Complexity, Political-Analyst, and Writing-for-Intel spine; old-to-new SEST crosswalks, Scenario Registry tags, Evidence Packet tags, Interagency Roles, Scoring, and Gates connect the registry to capstone and future method work.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because a research platform needs a deep technique inventory before it can recommend, combine, or test methods responsibly.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

instruments.operation: uses `Operations Research` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: National Security Policy through Intelligence and War, including the Research-Methods, Statistics, SAT, Operations Research, Game-Theory, Disruptive-Analytics, Forecasting, Modeling-Complexity, Political-Analyst, and Writing-for-Intel spine; old-to-new SEST crosswalks, Scenario Registry tags, Evidence Packet tags, Interagency Roles, Scoring, and Gates connect the registry to capstone and future method work.
instruments.output: a project-specific work product, decision, or proof surface tied to `Operations Research`
instruments.upstream_dependency: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations.
instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Look up a Georgetown SSP course by old OR new SEST code in index.csv. 2. Open the corresponding Part_ _SEST_ _*.md for the full lossless technique inventory.
instruments.proof: National Security Policy through Intelligence and War, including the Research-Methods, Statistics, SAT, Operations Research, Game-Theory, Disruptive-Analytics, Forecasting, Modeling-Complexity, Political-Analyst, and Writing-for-Intel spine; old-to-new SEST.
instruments.gap: Read the entry-point materials, classify the packet's authority level, and decide whether the next move is build, consolidation, archival retention, or public synthesis.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `Operations Research` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.

instruments.instrument_name: 50-course Georgetown SSP registry; 7,500+ technique lines; capstone plug tags [SR/EP/IR/SC/G] / Evidence Packet
instruments.input: project source text and the named component `Evidence Packet`
instruments.operation: uses `Evidence Packet` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: a parallel capstone_plugs.csv/.json maps technique lines into five capstone work product categories — [SR] Scenario Registry, [EP] Evidence Packets, [IR] Interagency Roles, [SC] Scoring, [G] Gates (QA/decision thresholds) — so any of the ~7,500+ technique lines can be... The mechanism is method operationalization: name the analytic technique, define its use condition, connect it to a case or judgment problem, record.

instruments.upstream_dependency: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations.
instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Look up a Georgetown SSP course by old OR new SEST code in index.csv. 2. Open the corresponding Part_ _SEST_ _*.md for the full lossless technique inventory.
instruments.proof: a parallel capstone_plugs.csv/.json maps technique lines into five capstone work product categories — [SR] Scenario Registry, [EP] Evidence Packets, [IR] Interagency Roles, [SC] Scoring, [G] Gates (QA/decision thresholds) — so any of the ~7,500+ technique.
instruments.gap: Read the entry-point materials, classify the packet's authority level, and decide whether the next move is build, consolidation, archival retention, or public synthesis.
